name: Popcakes
recipeInstructions:
1. In a mixing bowl, combine flour, salt, and baking soda.
2. In a small bowl, beat the egg until well mixed. Add the cooled butter to the egg and stir again.
3. Pour the egg mixture and buttermilk into the flour mixture. Stir briskly until well combined.
4. Fill 6 greased muffin cups about halfway full with the batter.
5. Bake at 425°F for 12-15 minutes, or until the popcakes are brown on top. Remove from the oven and serve immediately.
MEXICAN POPCAKES
Replace the flour with a mixture of 3/4 cup plus 1 tablespoon flour and 3 tablespoons masa harina. Follow the same recipe as above.
PANCAKES
Follow the Popcakes recipe, but barely stir the ingredients together so that the batter looks wet and shiny like a bowl of thick-curd cottage cheese. Don't worry about streaks of flour or lumps. Heat a skillet and melt just enough oil or shortening to cover the bottom. Drop heaping tablespoons of batter into the skillet and gently pat each mound down; don't flatten the clumps. Turn the popcakes as soon as the edges look dry, without waiting for bubbles to break on top.
Nutritional Information:
Each popcake contains about: 133 calories; 191 mg sodium; 47 mg cholesterol; 5 grams fat; 17 grams carbohydrates; 4 grams protein; 0.06 gram fiber.
